title: Axle and bearing for conveyor chain link
abstract: The invention relates to an axle (250) and bearing (130) for a conveyor chain link (24), such as those used in commercial bakery equipment. The conveyor chain link (24) generally includes at least one roller bearing wheel (130) with a central hole (138), an axle pin (250), and a link component (260) with a bore (262). The axle pin (250) is configured to extend through the hole (138) in the roller bearing wheel and the bore (262) in the link component to attach the at least one roller bearing wheel (130) to the link component (260). The axle pin (250) includes a medial portion (252) having at least one surface engagement feature (253) configured for anti-spin engagement with the link component (260). The roller bearing wheel (130) includes at least one engagement feature (146) for anti-spin engagement with the distal end (254, 256) of the axle pin (250).
claims: Claims What is Claimed is: 1 . A conveyor chain link comprising: a link component comprising a female engagement feature; an axle pin comprising an elongated generally cylindrical axle pin body having a medial portion and two distal ends, wherein the medial portion comprises at least one male engagement feature extending outward from the axle pin body, wherein the male engagement feature is configured for cooperative engagement with the female engagement feature of the link component. 2. The conveyor chain link of Claim 1 , wherein the male engagement feature comprises a rib extending outward from an outer surface of the cylindrical axle pin body. 3. The conveyor chain link of Claim 2, wherein the axle pin body defines a length and wherein the rib is positioned parallel to the length of the axle pin body. 4. The conveyor chain link of Claim 3, wherein the rib has a length and wherein the length of the rib is shorter than the length of the axle pin body. 5. The conveyor chain link of Claim 2, wherein the female engagement feature comprises a female slot configured to receive the male rib of the axle pin. 6. The conveyor chain link of Claim 5, wherein the link component comprises a bore and wherein the female slot is positioned on an outer periphery of the bore. 7. The conveyor chain link of Claim 6, wherein the medial portion of the axle body is positioned within the bore of the link component. 8. The conveyor chain link of Claim 1 , wherein the medial portion of the axle pin body is hardened to have a hardness greater than the link component. 9. The conveyor chain link of Claim 8, wherein the medial portion of the axle pin body is hardened to a depth of about 1/16 of an inch. 10. The conveyor chain link of Claim 1 , comprising at least one roller bearing wheel comprising at least one engagement feature, wherein the axle pin is configured to engage the at least one engagement feature of the roller bearing wheel. 1 1 . The conveyor chain link of Claim 10, wherein at least one distal end of the axle pin is configured to engage the at least one engagement feature of the roller bearing wheel. 12. A conveyor chain comprising a plurality of conveyor chain links according to Claim 1 , successively coupled to one another to form an elongate assembly allowing relative pivotal movement between adjacent links.
